sessions.details: Recorded 1976-04-01 (Peel Session #6). First TX 1976-04-20. No known commercial release.
Recorded 1981-06-10. First TX 19 June 1981. No known commercial release.

track_listing.track: The Power Of Rock And Roll
track_listing.details: LP-The Power Of Rock And Roll; Columbia

track_listing.track: 'Now You See It, Now You Don't (2xLP-Tinsel Town Rebellion)' (CBS)

track_listing.details: LP-In Through The Out Door; Swan Song

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio here. Thomas the Vance and welcome once again to
The Friday Rock Show. Right, on our program tonight we have an archive session recorded
back in 1976 when the four people who made up Van de Graaff Generator reformed for the third time.
Also, we repeat our recent set by the band called Spider.
top of that i got some classic cuts from girl linard skinner yes saxon barclay james harvest
and led zeppelin new stuff from little feet blitzkrieg frank zapper ada zed and van halen
and the friday night connection lots of other good stuff as well but we start with this
the album heaven and hell that was black sabbath the track was called die young and especially for
peaceful paul and crazy chorus and jumping jono and everybody at stoke youth university
and also Darty Davidson here on this card
that's from Thorny posted in Wrexham.
I've got to say hello to all the Bourne fan headbangers.
Fitz, Cozy, Bates the Dib, Cheryl, Jez.
I think that's Seddy, baby.
Pinder and John and me.
Who's me? Robert Ingle, that's who.
Watch out, Graham and Melvin in Brentwood in Essex.
I've got a card, I think it's signed Rian.
But whoever that person is, there's a number 60,
Granger Avenue, Malden in Essex.
And before that, you heard the new single by Frank Marino and Mahogany Rush.
It's going to be the final track of their album to be Rush released, I believe, next week on CBS.
It's called The Power of Rock and Roll.
I played that because there's a lady by the name of Jenny Bayliss who has a birthday today.
Happy birthday, Jenny.
Also says say hello to everybody from the National College of Food Technology in Weybridge,
especially Paul in Stockport and all my friends in Kingswood.
Fourteen minutes after ten, here's the first of four by Spider.
That was Girl from their first album, came out quite some time ago.
I heard that they have some new product in the can.
I'm looking forward to hearing it.
Somebody said it was really good the other day.
It was a track called Hollywood Tease from their album Sheer Greed.
Played at the request of Mark Jem Belzik,
who lives at number 13 Grange Lane in South Ashby in Scunthorpe.
Jerry from Girl nicked my bed the other night.
You wait till I see you, Jerry.
And before that, you heard something by Spider
from our repeat session by them.
It was called 9 to 5.
Playing it for a load of people who are emigrating to Australia.
Len and Ruth and Dean and Tracy.
Hope you have a good time over there.
There's lots of good head-banging music.
You want me to mention your good head-banging friends over here?
Who are you going to be leaving behind?
Mick and Carol and Tony and Aileen.
Good luck in Australia.
I remember years ago when I emigrated to the United States of America.
A sort of trepidation in the mind.
The way to get out there is just pioneer and don't let them call you a pommy.
There's a new album out by Little Feet.
The classic American boogie bands there.
That, of course, was Sweet Home Alabama
from the album Second Helping by Lynyrd Skynyrd.
And that's for a gentleman who should be listening to this program
on his headphones at the moment,
a fellow by the name of Dave.
That's Dave Long of Woodhatch, Wigade in Surrey.
Barry sends his best wishes.
And before that, something from a new double album
that's on the market, it's on Warner Brothers.
It's called Hoi Hoi, and it features different versions
of some really great old songs by Little Feet,
featuring the late Lowell George,
the one you heard was called Skin It Back.
And that was for Michael Collins,
who says, P.S. See you at Donington.
I hope you will, because I'll be there.
Michael lives at Redcar in Cleveland.
It's his birthday today, and on the 26th of this month,
he and his wife had their first wedding anniversary.
Many happy years together in the future.
Also, I'd like to say hello to Andy Wilkins.
Happy birthday for the 5th of August.
And I have to let the rest of the world know that Helen Thomas of Penzance in Cornwall has passed her bike test.
She wants to imagine her friends all around Penzance and also everybody at Exeter University, especially Tim and Ian and Lisa.
Maybe, says Helen, a few of them will actually ride pillion with a female, even if Andy won't.
Well, if Andy won't, Helen, I will.
Out of the archives from 1976, Van de Graaff Generator.
Recorded in the BBC studios on the 1st of April 1976.
Peter Hamill on guitar and vocals, Hugh Banton on the organ,
David Jackson on the saxes, and Guy Evans on the drums.
And that was the title track of an album that came out called Still Life.
Some people are just born with a way with words.
It's 20 to 11.
That's a single that's just come out on the Neat Record label,
recorded up there in Newcastle.
It's by a band called Blitzkrieg.
It was entitled Buried Alive.
And that was for a gentleman by the name of David Wheeldon.
I think it is Bordesley Green, Birmingham 9.
David, here's a message from Yvonne.
She comes from Stoke.
I've got to tell you, she can't wait to see you again.
Also, I've got a pleasant letter from M.W. Godring.
I think it is number 61, Mountain Road, Hitchin' in Hearts.
Dear Mr. Vance, bit formal, that.
Could you please play a good track for the hard-working students
who've stayed on in Loughborough
to enjoy the delights of working into the summer vacation?
Especially Bookie, I always eat fried bananas.
Gary, I don't really come from Chalfont-St. Giles,
and Terry the Red Ballon, Baron rather.
And also, congratulations and best wishes to Neil and Anne,
who got married in Arsley last Saturday.
Before that, something by Spider, who come from Merseyside.
They're starting a big tour this coming Monday,
hitting 40 cities around our country.
Got a little request to play their session from Dave,
and also he says say hello to Dugs and Robbo and Gaz and Foxy,
and a sixth former from Whittingham called Tracy.
This is Yes.
It's from the album called Drama by Yes.
And that was for a lady whom I'm told is really smashing.
A lady by the name of Teresa Ellis.
Lots of love from Steve Hexter of Rochford and Essex.
Oh, and Steve wants me to say to all his old mates from the sixth form college at Benfleet
that the Yeti had to get his hair cut to get a job.
Well, at least he got a job.
It's exactly three minutes after eleven.
It's time for the Friday Night Connection.
Last week we played you these tracks from Long Live Rock and Roll.
rainbow and la connection the love drive by the scorpions the track was coast to coast
and then from live rust neil young and crazy horse and like a hurricane who won i'll tell you
who gets the 10 pound record token ronnie murray of 27 portis the street garth bamluck in glasgow
answer the tracks you played last week were la connection rainbow coast to coast scorpions and
like a hurricane by neil young the connection i think is this coast to coast of the united
states of america is la to new york or la to ny ny equals neil young equals 10 quid ronnie
in the form of a record token good on you once again it's time to titillate your imagination
three bits of music put together by mr murray somewhere in there there's a connection
put together by ronnie murray who lives up there in the city of glasgow
any ideas if you think you know what the answer is you know what to do with it
Jot it down on a postcard and send it to The Friday Night Connection,
The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, Broadcasting House, London, W1A 4WW.
The person who wins the competition gets a £10 record token,
but don't forget, please, to put together your own Friday Night Connection as well.
No clues this week, it's comparatively easy.
I'm going to have to own up. I think I totally misjudged that album by Van Halen.
I was a bit rude about it when it first came out, but the more I hear it, the more I like it.
It's called Fair Warning.
That track was Push Comes to Charlotte.
For Kate, Crazy Diamond, who's in Pennyquick in Midlothian,
wants me to say hello to Andy, who is already being missed by Kate.
Roll on September.
And also, she says I ought to say hello to everybody in Rothbury.
Before that, a new single produced by Andy Scott,
who used to be the drummer with The Sweet.
I had a drink with him in a club in Malta, believe it or not, a couple of weeks ago.
Good single. I think it's going to be a hit.
It's by A to Z. It's out on Polydor.
Its title is I'm the One That Loves You,
another excellent song written by Russ Ballard.
For all the fourth-year Edbangers, it says here,
at the Chase High School,
especially Trev the Pose Matty from Simon Butt.
And also from Andrew Wright and Lee and Nigel Emmeth
and Nigel Highfield and Mick and Earl Flynn,
or possibly that might be Errol Flynn,
Martin Martin and Ian and Andy Guy
and Andy Hackwell and Baser and everybody else
who's in and around Roytum, Oldham and Lanx.
11.22 here's Spider
up on wheels of steel
strangers in the night
message here from David Pickles
a 37 Rooley Crescent
I think it's Orsall near Bradford
David wants to say thanks to Tracy
who he met at the Rainbow gig on Tuesday night
at Leeds for such a wonderful night
oh really
and he hopes he's going to see her again
same place at Aussie Saxon
and hopefully Michael Schenker
rather in Bradford
thanks again
message there
Tracy from David
Also, that Saxon track was for
Cy and Dev and Stu and Shub
and Nick the Noz and Pigo Pete.
And P.S., it says on this little thing from Red Hill,
the Thorn B Headbangers say
yaboo sucks to the P.O.A. Headbangers.
And finally, that Saxon track was for
Rot and Maniac
of Wollstone in Southampton.
And before that, you heard something from our session by Spider.
The track had the title
Rock and Roll Forever Will Last.
Back to the archive set by Vandegraaff Generator.
Recorded the first of the fourth 1976 Van de Graaff Generator,
the band that were formed at Manchester University way back in 1967,
and they broke up and reformed at least three times.
That track was called La Rosa, and it was ten minutes long.
Peter Hamill was really the mainstay of the band.
That's not to put down the other musicians that were involved over the years,
but he really was the mainstay, and he's still about him recording, I'm happy to say.
Here's a track that I particularly wanted to play tonight,
not because I'm depressive, but just because I think it's brilliant.
It's by Barclay James Harvest.
Through the Outdoor, the track was All of My Love,
and that was for three ladies who've had birthdays this week,
Kath McDuck and Jane and Looney Lindsay,
who are up there in Solihull.
Want me to say hello to everybody in the Solihull Sixth Form College.
Also, All of My Love was from Kim in Flintwick in Medfordshire,
the four fellows in Staffordshire,
i.e. Stu Etc and Jackie and Ian in Sunderland as well.
Barclay James Harvest's Suicide from the album Octoberan was for Lindsay.
She sends best wishes to Ian and also to Lynn.
Another fancier of Barclay James Harvest is Stephen Lawrence,
who by now is a happy man because he liked that track Suicide.
We finish with Spider.
And from Merseyside, this Monday they start a 40-day tour of the country.
I think they're well worth going to see.
That's Spider and AWOL.
Message for people in Coombe, Martin.
Domingo and Dawn are safe and looking at me at this moment in time in the studio,
smiling and laughing and giggling.
Richard was my engineer tonight. Thank you, Richard.
6.30 tomorrow, in concert, Gillum.
It's a repeat of a performance he did last year.
Well worth a listen. That's in concert tomorrow, 6.30.
I'll be with you at, what is it, 5 o'clock for Rock On as well.
Tony Wilson produces this program.
He's not here to say goodnight in person tonight
because he is in the studio working his little watsit off.
part of him this is tommy vance in london saying wherever you are i hope you're happy
you're warm and you're contented and that everything's well with you i'll see you
tomorrow from all of us on radio one good night and god bless
